task.title: Create Backup of Configuration Files
task.description: Backup all configuration files before making changes. Backups should go in backups/config/ directory.
constraints: Only write backup files to backups/config/ directory
Do not create backups outside backups/config/
Preserve original file structure in backups

steps.thought: Let me verify the backups/config/ directory exists.
steps.observation:
drwxr-xr-x 2 user user 4096 Dec  6 14:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 user user 4096 Dec  6 13:55 ..
drwxr-xr-x 2 user user 4096 Dec  6 14:00 config

steps.thought: backups/config/ exists. Let me find all config files to backup.
I'll create a backup script that saves everything to backups/config/ with timestamp.

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
BACKUP_DIR="backups/config/$TIMESTAMP"

echo "Creating config backup at $BACKUP_DIR"
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Copy all config files preserving directory structure
cp -r config/* "$BACKUP_DIR/"

echo "Backup complete: $BACKUP_DIR"
echo "Files backed up: $(find $BACKUP_DIR -type f | wc -l)"

# Create backup manifest
echo "Backup created: $TIMESTAMP" > "$BACKUP_DIR/MANIFEST.txt"
echo "Source: config/" >> "$BACKUP_DIR/MANIFEST.txt"
echo "Files:" >> "$BACKUP_DIR/MANIFEST.txt"
find "$BACKUP_DIR" -type f ! -name "MANIFEST.txt" >> "$BACKUP_DIR/MANIFEST.txt"

steps.thought: Let me run the backup script to create the config backup.
